ken-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled

ken-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
ken-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: Easily hitconfirmable into Target Combo; farthest ~40% of hitbox is shifted upward (can whiff against some crouching attack hurtboxes in whiff punish scenarios); extends a forward leg hurtbox until end of recovery; Run~Stop cancel: -6/-12

ken-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
ken-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Great poke in neutral; large number of active frames make it a great meaty; when used out of Drive Rush, links into 2MK on hit or gives pressure on block; Run~Stop cancel: -3/-8

ken-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
ken-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Incredible range for a light normal and a great whiff punish; Run~Stop cancel: -14/-16; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

ken-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
ken-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Has good forward movement; whiffs on crouching opponents at farther ranges unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up makes it strong in neutral; Super is much harder to hitconfirm but can buffer into a safe SA2 in neutral; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 4-15, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

ken-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: 2 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
Forces a spinning knockdown on Punish Counter
ken-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Excellent ranged poke and whiff punish tool; even at max range, can convert Punish Counter spinning juggle state into 623KK or Drive Rush 5HP juggles; 2 extra recovery frames on whiff; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

ken-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled
ken-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Primary combo starter; chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Run~Stop cancel: -12/-18; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

ken-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
ken-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: Good buffer tool and great for pressure out of Drive Rush; Run~Stop cancel: -8/-11; 2f extra recovery on whiff and extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-8 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

ken-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
notes: Can be canceled until the 2nd active frame
ken-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand; decent anti-air (cannot hit cross-up); only first 2 active frames are cancelable; cancel into 236HK on block leaves a 5f gap and is DI-safe; Run~Stop cancel: +3/-7

ken-crouch-light-kick.name: Crouching Light Kick
notes: Can be rapid canceled

ken-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
ken-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Run~Stop cancel: -8/-12; Range: 1.46; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

ken-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Forces a Hard Knock Down when hitting an opponent on the ground
Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
1 frame of recovery added when blocked

ken-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
ken-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; cancels to j.214K with a long cancel window; lower body projectile invuln 6-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ken-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a slam knockdown on counter or Punish Counter
ken-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Spike Knockdown on counter-hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; great downward hitbox for close jump-ins and neutral jump throw baits; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ken-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties

ken-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
notes: Has cross-up attack properties
ken-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

ken-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
ken-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Great horizontal range for a jump-in; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

quick-dash.name: Quick Dash
notes: Swiftly sprint a set distance forward
Can transition to branching attacks from frame 12
quick-dash.notes_sc: Can input follow-ups from 11-31f; follow-ups will not reverse direction if crossing under opponent; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration; when inputting with 5KKK takes priority over OD special moves (does not apply when inputting with 5KK after performing a special move input); Run Distance: 1.37 (early stop) / 2.92 (max)

ken-emergency-stop.name: Emergency Stop
notes: During Quick Dash
Performed from Quick Dash, this maneuver will make you stop dead in your tracks
ken-emergency-stop.notes_sc: 27f is fastest total Run~Stop time; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration

thunder-kick.name: Thunder Kick
notes: During Quick Dash
An overhead attack performed from Quick Dash that cannot be blocked crouching
Can be canceled into Level 3 Super Art or Critical Art
thunder-kick.notes_sc: Ken's only overhead outside of Jinrai Kick follow-ups; notably can't benefit from Drive Rush like other overheads, but can be used in meaty setups (+4) to link afterwards; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 10-20, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

forward-step-kick.name: Forward Step Kick
notes: During Quick Dash
A forward kick with long reach that is performed from Quick Dash
Can be canceled into any Super Art
forward-step-kick.notes_sc: Acts like a Normal Attack for Super canceling (including no extra damage scaling when canceled to SA3); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can be spaced safely in neutral, or used as a frame trap out of 5HP~Run (allowing a link on counter-hit); extends a forward hurtbox during recovery

chin-buster-2nd.name: Chin Buster
notes: A quick series of attacks that can be canceled into special moves
chin-buster-2nd.notes_sc: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; Run~Stop cancel: KD+30/-12

triple-flash-kick-2.name: Triple Flash Kicks (2)
notes: A sequence of three kicks with long reach

triple-flash-kick-3.name: Triple Flash Kicks (3)
notes: A sequence of three kicks with long reach
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

ken-hadoken-light.name: Hadoken (Light)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ken-hadoken-medium.name: Hadoken (Medium)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ken-hadoken-heavy.name: Hadoken (Heavy)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ken-hadoken-od.name: Hadoken (OD)
notes: Fire a ki blast directly forward. Useful for attacking opponents at range

ken-shoryuken-light.name: Shoryuken (Light)
notes: Leap into the sky with a devastating uppercut. Useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to end combos
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 35
Does 800 individual damage from the 3rd active frame and on

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14

ken-shoryuken-medium.name: Shoryuken (Medium)
notes: Leap into the sky with a devastating uppercut. Useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to end combos
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 43

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8

ken-shoryuken-heavy.name: Shoryuken (Heavy)
notes: Leap into the sky with a devastating uppercut. Useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to end combos
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9
Invincible to projectiles from frames 5 - 11
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 51

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 9

ken-shoryuken-od.name: Shoryuken (OD)
notes: Leap into the sky with a devastating uppercut. Useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to end combos
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 10
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 24 and 33 - 74

quick-dash-shoryuken.name: [Quick Dash] Shoryuken
notes: During Quick Dash
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 18
Invincible to projectiles from frames 3 - 11
Considered airborne from frames 11 - 51

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 18

ken-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-light.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Light)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos
Considered airborne from frames 5 - 28

ken-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-medium.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Medium)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 41

ken-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-heavy.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (Heavy)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 61

ken-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-od.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: Unleash a series of roundhouse kicks while moving forward. Useful when canceled into from normal moves to create combos
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 77

quick-dash-tatsumaki-senpukyaku.name: [Quick Dash] Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
notes: During Quick Dash
Does not hit the opponent in front of you on the 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, and 10th hits
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 61

ken-aerial-tatsumaki-senpukyaku.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
notes: A Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku performed while airborne. Useful for altering the trajectory of your jump, or for staging a surprise attack from above
ken-aerial-tatsumaki-senpukyaku.notes_sc: Forward jump only; 7f extra landing recovery on whiff; can hit twice vs. airborne opponents; can hit cross-up (deals 5f less hitstun, 200 less damage); Ken is in a crouching state from 4th landing recovery frame onward

ken-aerial-tatsumaki-senpukyaku-od.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku (OD)
notes: A Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku performed while airborne. Useful for altering the trajectory of your jump, or for staging a surprise attack from above
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 77
Does not hit the opponent in front of you on the 2nd and 4th attacks
(The 2nd and 4th attacks do 700 damage and increases the Super Art gauge by 600)
7 frames of recovery added when the attack misses

dragonlash-kick-light.name: Dragonlash Kick (Light)
notes: A leaping roundhouse kick that pulls your opponent toward you. An extremely versatile move that can be used to launch surprise attacks from mid-range, as a way to continue your offensive pressure, or in combos
Does not hit opponents on the ground from frames 18 - 20
Does 950 damage from frames 21 - 23
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 28

dragonlash-kick-medium.name: Dragonlash Kick (Medium)
notes: A leaping roundhouse kick that pulls your opponent toward you. An extremely versatile move that can be used to launch surprise attacks from mid-range, as a way to continue your offensive pressure, or in combos
Does not hit opponents on the ground from frames 23 - 24
Considered airborne from frames 14 - 32

dragonlash-kick-heavy.name: Dragonlash Kick (Heavy)
notes: A leaping roundhouse kick that pulls your opponent toward you. An extremely versatile move that can be used to launch surprise attacks from mid-range, as a way to continue your offensive pressure, or in combos
Does not hit opponents on the ground from frames 28 - 29
Considered airborne from frames 19 - 37

dragonlash-kick-od.name: Dragonlash Kick (OD)
notes: A leaping roundhouse kick that pulls your opponent toward you. An extremely versatile move that can be used to launch surprise attacks from mid-range, as a way to continue your offensive pressure, or in combos
Does not hit opponents on the ground from frames 9 - 12 and 18
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 27
Does not have additional combo scaling if the 2nd attack hits

quick-dash-dragonlash-kick.name: [Quick Dash] Dragonlash Kick
notes: During Quick Dash
Does not hit opponents on the ground from frames 9 - 12 and 18 - 19
Considered airborne from frames 10 - 28
Does not have additional combo scaling if the 2nd attack hits

jinrai-kick-light.name: Jinrai Kick (Light)
notes: A forward-moving roundhouse kick that can be followed up by one of three different kicks
Can transition to Kazekama Shin Kick and Gorai Axe Kick from frames 32 - 35
Can transition to Senka Snap Kick from frames 30 - 35

jinrai-kick-medium.name: Jinrai Kick (Medium)
notes: A forward-moving roundhouse kick that can be followed up by one of three different kicks
Can transition to Kazekama Shin Kick and Gorai Axe Kick from frames 32 - 35
Can transition to Senka Snap Kick from frames 30 - 35

jinrai-kick-heavy.name: Jinrai Kick (Heavy)
notes: A forward-moving roundhouse kick that can be followed up by one of three different kicks
Can transition to Kazekama Shin Kick and Gorai Axe Kick from frames 36 - 41
Can transition to Senka Snap Kick from frames 34 - 41

kazekama-shin-kick.name: Kazekama Shin Kick
notes: During Jinrai Kick
A low kick performed from Jinrai Kick that cannot be blocked standing. Useful against opponents who try to move after your Jinrai Kick
Always in Punish Counter state during execution
kazekama-shin-kick.notes_sc: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; using 236MK~6LK will counter-hit any opponent button; causes a +32 Hard Knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (gives good oki but prevents follow-up links)

gorai-axe-kick.name: Gorai Axe Kick
notes: During Jinrai Kick
An axe kick performed from Jinrai Kick that cannot be blocked crouching. Useful against opponents who try to stiffen their guard against your Jinrai Kick
Always in Punish Counter state during execution
Adds a floor bounding effect during a mid-air hit
gorai-axe-kick.notes_sc: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 4-21, allowing it to dodge some low attacks; due to its slow startup, opponent can easily switch from low block to high block to cover both mixup options; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG bounce state (can only follow up with SA3 cancel if the juggle was started with the 6MK follow-up)

senka-snap-kick.name: Senka Snap Kick
notes: During Jinrai Kick
A roundhouse that can be used to combo from Jinrai Kick. Effective when used to make a combo after landing a Jinrai Kick
Always in Punish Counter state during execution
Does not hit on crouching opponents
senka-snap-kick.notes_sc: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; better KD Adv. after 236HK launch; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox (236LK '''-23/-35'''; 236MK '''-22/-31'''; 236HK KD/'''-26''')

jinrai-kick-od.name: Jinrai Kick (OD)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD
A forward-moving roundhouse kick that can be followed up by one of three different kicks
Can transition to Overdrive Kazekama Shin Kick and Overdrive Gorai Axe Kick from frames 21 - 27
Can transition to Overdrive Senka Snap Kick from frames 19 - 27

kazekama-shin-kick-od.name: Kazekama Shin Kick (OD)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD
A low kick performed from Jinrai Kick that cannot be blocked standing. Useful against opponents who try to move after your Jinrai Kick
Can transition to Kasai Thrust Kick from frame 11
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

kasai-thrust-kick-1.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (1)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD or Kazekam Shin Kick OD
A sequence of kicks performed following any type of OD Jinrai Kick. Useful for slipping through your opponent's guard to tack on additional damage
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

gorai-axe-kick-od.name: Gorai Axe Kick (OD)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD
An axe kick performed from Jinrai Kick that cannot be blocked crouching. Useful against opponents who try to stiffen their guard against your Jinrai Kick
Can transition to Kasai Thrust Kick from frame 11
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

kasai-thrust-kick-2.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (2)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD or Gorai Axe Kick OD
A sequence of kicks performed following any type of OD Jinrai Kick. Useful for slipping through your opponent's guard to tack on additional damage
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

senka-snap-kick-od.name: Senka Snap Kick (OD)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD
A roundhouse that can be used to combo from Jinrai Kick. Effective when used to make a combo after landing a Jinrai Kick
Can transition to Kasai Thrust Kick from frame 11
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

kasai-thrust-kick.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (3)
notes: During Jinrai Kick OD or Senka Snap Kick OD
A sequence of kicks performed following any type of OD Jinrai Kick. Useful for slipping through your opponent's guard to tack on additional damage
Always in Punish Counter state during execution

dragonlash-flame.name: Dragonlash Flame
notes: Launch your opponent into the sky and then perform a series of kicks on them while airborne. It is invincible at the beginning, and you switch positions with your opponent upon completion
Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 9
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 30%

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws on frames 1 - 9

dragonlash-flame.notes_sc: Switches sides with opponent on hit; Full Dmg distribution: 500,300x2,900

shippu-jinrai-kyaku.name: Shippu Jinrai-kyaku
notes: Unleash a flurry of kicks and then finish with a rising Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku. This move is quick to execute, making it useful for counterattacks and in combos
Transitions to the full attack after the 6th attack
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 40%
shippu-jinrai-kyaku.notes_sc: Fast startup and good range, making it good for punishes and juggle combos; completely safe on block due to pushback; great chip tool vs. opponents in Burnout (can continue dealing more chip after being safely blocked); benefits from 20% Counter-hit/Punish Counter damage increase unlike most Supers; Full Dmg distribution: 300x4,200x5,600

shinryu-reppa.name: Shinryu Reppa
notes: A one-two Shoryuken combination followed by one final mighty Shoryuken with an added spin to it. If the first hit misses, a single additional Shoryuken will come out afterward
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 18
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 50%
Following properties occur if the first attack is blocked or misses
- Damage is 2100
- Reduces Drive Gauge by 9000 on hit
- Does not go into full cinematic animation
Can only be canceled into from special moves
shinryu-reppa.notes_sc: 1st hit leads to full animation (non-cinematic damage varies based on juggle height, and allows a follow-up juggle); 2 hits whiff on crouch block (reduced chip, slightly worse advantage); cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Ken

shinryu-reppa-ca.name: Shinryu Reppa (Critical Art)
notes: When under 25% vitality
A one-two Shoryuken combination followed by one final mighty Shoryuken with an added spin to it. If the first hit misses, a single additional Shoryuken will come out afterward
Completely invincible from frames 1 - 18
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 50%
Following properties occur if the first attack is blocked or misses
- Damage is 2200
- Reduces Drive Gauge by 16000 on hit
- Does not go into full cinematic animation
Can only be canceled into from special moves

knee-strikes.name: Knee Strikes
notes: When near opponent
Grab your opponent, disrupt their balance with a knee strike, and toss them forward. Throws your opponent without switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
knee-strikes.notes_sc: Can walk forward for a corner throw loop with 1f timing vs. standard size characters (2f vs. Marisa, 3f vs. Blanka/E.Honda, 4f vs. Zangief); can also Drive Rush for easy manual timing (can whiff DR~5LP to bait 5f reversals, but is punished by reversal 4f lights); only +1 after forward dash (can pressure with jabs, but throw loses to 4f); can use Drive Rush meaty normals after a midscreen throw; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

hell-wheel.name: Hell Wheel
notes: When near opponent
Grab your opponent and roll backwards with your foot placed on their torso. Throws your opponent while switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
hell-wheel.notes_sc: Side switches, has great corner carry; does not allow throw/strike oki after back throwing opponent into corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
